subject: Sports
title: Mike Powell: The Long Jump Record
description: A quick win: understand Mike Powell: The Long Jump Record.

hook.text: 8.95 meters. Mike Powell's long jump from 1991 beat Bob Beamon's 'unbreakable' record that had stood for 23 years.

buildup.text: Bob Beamon's 8.90m jump at the 1968 Mexico City Olympics was so far ahead of its time that many called it permanent.

discovery.text: Powell leapt 8.95m at the 1991 Tokyo World Championships in a head-to-head duel with Carl Lewis.

twist.text: Lewis actually jumped 8.91m in the same competition—beating Beamon's record—but still lost to Powell.

climax.text: Powell's record has now stood for over 30 years. The pattern repeats: each long jump record seems unbreakable.

punchline.text: He beat an unbreakable record. Now his record looks just as permanent.

quiz.question: Whose 'unbreakable' record did Mike Powell surpass?
